message:
feat(workflows): tick 186 check_step_idempotency cx-threaded (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Ninth tick of the workflows/runner.rs strangler-fig. Closes the
last non-trait storage seam inside run_workflow_inner.

New helpers in workflows/plan_helpers.rs:
- `check_step_idempotency_with_cx` — routes the single storage
  call (get_step_logs → get_step_logs_with_cx). On cx-cancel,
  returns `NotExecuted` matching the legacy "storage error →
  NotExecuted" degrade contract. Cancellation then surfaces at
  the next `cx.checkpoint()` (e.g. tick 181's per-step seam) —
  the idempotency check itself doesn't abort.
- `check_step_idempotency_maybe_cx` dispatcher.

Call-site migration at L693 in run_workflow_inner (the pre-step
idempotency check before each iteration's step.execute).

Every non-trait storage call on run_workflow_inner's execution
path now threads cx when cx is Some. Only remaining work:
- `step.execute(&mut ctx).await` — needs Step trait change
  (invasive; would need Step::execute_with_cx or similar)
- 2 legacy callers of `run_workflow` (cross-crate)

Verified via fork bypass: 31/31 workflows::runner:: tests pass.

Strangler-fig progress:
- ✅ 178 entry shim
- ✅ 179 engine-helper siblings
- ✅ 180 startup handshake (4 sites)
- ✅ 181 per-step cancel seam
- ✅ 182 update_execution_step (4 sites)
- ✅ 183 fail_execution (7 sites)
- ✅ 184 remaining lifecycle helpers (12 sites)
- ✅ 185 record_workflow_terminal_action (14 sites)
- ✅ 186 check_step_idempotency (1 site) — this

Total: 55+ cx-threaded storage seams across 9 ticks in the
workflows/runner subsystem. The cx-first chain inside
`run_workflow_inner` is now complete for every storage-direct
call. Remaining deep work (Step trait, 2 cross-crate callers)
sits at architectural boundaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
